title: lupinus citrinus var. deflexus
intro: Lupinus citrinus var. deflexus, also known as the dwarf or yellow bush lupine, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrub that produces yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Lupinus citrinus var. deflexus can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
